pages: Hi Annika, this is your experiment!

In this experiment you will be presented with a series of words that progress automatically.
You will need to remember the sequence.
On each trial, decide whether the word is the same as the one two (2) words ago (M) or not (Z).

Let's have a short practice. Each trial will present one word.
You must decide whether the word matches the one presented two (2) trials ago.

If the word matches, press the 'm' key.
If the word does not match, press the 'z' key.

You will receive feedback after the block is finished.
Words can repeat, so try to remember the sequence not just recognise words.

pages.title: Ready to begin
pages.text: Click the button below to start the study in fullscreen mode.

instructions.endPages:
That concludes the experiment.
Thank you very much for participating.

Please read the debrief form on the next screen, then click Continue and Done so your completion can be finalized.

values.cell1Intro:
Press SPACE for PM items from the category: animals.

For all other words you will respond to the n-back task as usual:

Press 'm' when the word is the same as two (2) back ago.
Otherwise, press 'z'.
values.cell1PreScreen:
In the following blocks you will also perform a task we call "PM" (Prospective Memory).
The PM task requires you to press the spacebar on trials where a word belongs to a certain category.
You will be told what categories require a PM response before the block.
It is important that you remember to press the spacebar whenever you see a word belonging to those categories.
values.cell2Intro:
Press SPACE for PM items from any of the categories: colours, flowers, fruit/vegetables.

For all other words you will respond to the n-back task as usual:

Press 'm' when the word is the same as two (2) back ago.
Otherwise, press 'z'.
values.cell2PostScreen:
There will be no more PM trials in the experiment.
For the remaining trials, please complete the n-back task as usual.
You do not need to look for any word categories.
values.cell3Intro:
Press 'm' when the word is the same as two (2) back ago.
Otherwise, press 'z'.

variables.blockIntro:
Remember, trials will proceed automatically, but you must respond to each word.

Press 'm' if the word matches the one two (2) trials ago.
Press 'z' if the word does not match.
The first two words will require a 'z' response, because there are no corresponding two-back trials.

blocks.label: Practice Block
afterBlockScreens:
Great work! The rest of the experiment will always use this same response rule.
You are always responding to whether the current word matches the one from two (2) trials ago.
Words can repeat, so try to remember the sequence not just recognise words.
repeatAfterBlockScreens:
Remember:
Press 'm' when the word is the same as two (2) back ago.
Otherwise, press 'z'.

Press continue when you are ready for the next set of trials.

blocks.label: Block 3
afterBlockScreens:
You are halfway through the experiment.
Take a short breath before continuing.
